title: T-086 — UI: TEC-025 BOM Snapshots Viewer spec-driven screen
prompt:
# T-086 — UI: TEC-025 BOM Snapshots Viewer (spec-driven)

Implement the MVP UI task for TEC-025 BOM Snapshots Viewer. Exact canonical prototype is missing, so this is a spec-driven UI task: `docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md` §7.5 / spec-driven immutable snapshot viewer and `prototypes/design/03-TECHNICAL-UX.md` are authoritative; adjacent Technical prototype elements may be reused only as layout/primitives, not as a false 1:1 source. Route target: `/technical/boms/snapshots`. Apply 03-TECHNICAL red-lines (FG/WIP, factory_specs, shared BOM SSOT, Quality lab ownership, D365 optional) where applicable. Closeout requires screenshot/artifacts plus Playwright traces/artifacts per shared UI policy.

pipeline_inputs.description: UI: TEC-025 BOM Snapshots Viewer spec-driven screen
pipeline_inputs.details:
Spec-driven because no exact dedicated snapshot viewer prototype exists. Implement immutable BOM snapshot list/viewer: filters by work order/item/date, snapshot metadata, flattened header/lines/co-products, and a JSON/field diff modal. The BOM detail Snapshot History tab is an adjacent reference only. Released snapshots are read-only evidence and must not mutate current BOM.

Additional acceptance detail preserved from compressed AC list:
- Given closeout is prepared, screenshots/artifacts and Playwright traces/artifacts where applicable are listed per shared UI policy.
- UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Given the UI surface is implemented, when compared with the dedicated layout-primitive prototype `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/spec-driven-screens.jsx:223-303` (`bom_snapshots_viewer_screen` immutable list filterable by WO) plus its companion modal `:307-354` (`bom_snapshot_diff_modal` JSON-flatten diff vs current canonical BOM), then it satisfies structural parity, visual parity, and interaction parity against the PRD §7.5 / UX-spec snapshot-viewer layout/flow (PRD/UX is canonical, prototype is layout-primitive only), captures Playwright screenshots and traces, passes axe checks, and applies the Technical red-line overlay: FG not FA, WIP/intermediate not PR-code, factory_spec/internal_product_spec, shared BOM SSOT, and D365 optional only. Snapshots are immutable and must not mutate current BOM rows; orphaned snapshots remain read-only.
Given TEC-025 renders, then it satisfies the named PRD/UX spec as source of truth and does not claim exact prototype parity.
Given adjacent prototype primitives are reused, then screen semantics/routes/fields/states remain those required by PRD/UX.

Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect the task evidence, then these additional requirements are proven together: Given closeout is prepared, screenshots/artifacts and Playwright traces/artifacts where applicable are listed per shared UI policy.; UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
test_strategy: RED RTL/Playwright tests for PRD/UX spec-driven structure and interactions.
Run the relevant UI test for TEC-025; capture screenshot and trace artifacts.
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
risk_red_lines: Do not block on missing prototype; use spec-driven contract explicitly.
Do not use adjacent prototype as 1:1 source.
